== name in kana (P1814) == * おぐらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8130005006880 == Commons category (P373) == * Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府宇治市小倉町寺内31', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 巨椋神社 {{commons category|Category:Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto)}} [[JA:巨椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63148006}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}Ogura-chō, Terauchi 31| |画像=[[File:Ogura_Jinja_Uji_Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |創建=Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|n|6027901792}} [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ogura Shrine'''（おぐらじんじゃ）is a shrine in Terauchi, Ogura-chō, {{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is thought to have been the clan shrine of the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji, and is said to have begun when they enshrined their ancestral deity{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Kunihiko Shimonaka |title=Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1981/03/13 |page=108 |isbn=}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}（たけみかづちのかみ） * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}（ふつぬしのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}（あめのこやねのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}}（ひめのおおかみ） * Tutelary deity of the Ogura area === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}, but it is more likely that it originated when the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji enshrined its ancestral deity. Ogura-no-Muraji is an ancient clan listed under "Divine descent" in the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}. === Outline === In the late Heian period it became a place of worship for the {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|WD=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}, became a Kasuga-line shrine, and was called Kasuga Shrine until early modern times. There is a precinct shrine, Komori Shrine (Uji). It originally stood at Kasuga-no-mori in Ogura-chō, and was later moved to its current location{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=283 |isbn=}}. === Precincts === In the precincts are giant hackberry and zelkova trees said to be at least 700-800 years old{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 6: Life and Environment in Western Uji River Area |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1981/03/01 |page=514 |isbn=}}. === Komori Shrine === [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Keidaisha Komori Jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Komori Shrine honden}}]] ==== Enshrined deities ==== Hiruko-no-Kami（ひるこのかみ） Ame-no-Iwakusubune-no-Kami（あめのいわくすぶねのかみ） ==== History ==== It is said that Prince Koretaka, after defeating three giant kites that attacked children, built a shrine in Ogura on his way back from Kawachi. Since then, it came to be revered as a protector of children, thus "Komori Shrine." Today, the main shrine Ogura Shrine is often mistaken for Komori Shrine, but Komori Shrine exists separately as a precinct shrine within Ogura Shrine{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=284 |isbn=}}. === Monument of Gyokuro Tea Origin === Within the precincts is a "Monument of the Origin of Gyokuro Tea Production," based on an anecdote about the birth of {{ill|lt=Gyokuro|Q1424874|WD=Q1424874|1=Gyokuro}} by Yamamoto Kahei{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/nosan/documents/1312184290019.pdf|title=Tour of the Kyoto-Yamashiro Uji tea region|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2022-06-13}}. === Gallery === Image:Ogura komainu 02.jpg|Cover hall（honden inside） Image:Ogura Jinja Uji Honden.jpg|Honden Image:Ogura komainu 01.jpg|Ogura Shrine komainu Image:Ogura Shrine 20190309.jpg|Precincts and torii of Ogura Shrine Image:Komori Shrine Torii 20190309.jpg|Torii of Komori Shrine === Address === Kyoto Prefecture, Uji City, Ogura-chō Terauchi 31{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=Origins of Komori Shrine |publisher=Komori Shrine office |year= |page= |isbn=}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: about 10 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Ogura Station (Kyoto)|Q7080297|WD=Q7080297|lt=Ogura Station|1=Ogura Station}}. * {{ill|lt=JR Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}}: about 14 minutes on foot north from {{ill|lt=JR Ogura Station|Q1049411|WD=Q1049411|1=JR Ogura Station}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Komori Shrine|Q11447878|WD=Q11447878|1=Komori Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogura Shrine (Uji)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogura Shrine (Uji)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:宇治市の建築物]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おぐらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8130005006880 == Commons category (P373) == * Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府宇治市小倉町寺内31', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 巨椋神社 {{commons category|Category:Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto)}} [[JA:巨椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63148006}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}Ogura-chō, Terauchi 31| |画像=[[File:Ogura_Jinja_Uji_Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |創建=Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|n|6027901792}} [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ogura Shrine'''（おぐらじんじゃ）is a shrine in Terauchi, Ogura-chō, {{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is thought to have been the clan shrine of the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji, and is said to have begun when they enshrined their ancestral deity{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Kunihiko Shimonaka |title=Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1981/03/13 |page=108 |isbn=}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}（たけみかづちのかみ） * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}（ふつぬしのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}（あめのこやねのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}}（ひめのおおかみ） * Tutelary deity of the Ogura area === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}, but it is more likely that it originated when the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji enshrined its ancestral deity. Ogura-no-Muraji is an ancient clan listed under "Divine descent" in the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}. === Outline === In the late Heian period it became a place of worship for the {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|WD=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}, became a Kasuga-line shrine, and was called Kasuga Shrine until early modern times. There is a precinct shrine, Komori Shrine (Uji). It originally stood at Kasuga-no-mori in Ogura-chō, and was later moved to its current location{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=283 |isbn=}}. === Precincts === In the precincts are giant hackberry and zelkova trees said to be at least 700-800 years old{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 6: Life and Environment in Western Uji River Area |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1981/03/01 |page=514 |isbn=}}. === Komori Shrine === [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Keidaisha Komori Jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Komori Shrine honden}}]] ==== Enshrined deities ==== Hiruko-no-Kami（ひるこのかみ） Ame-no-Iwakusubune-no-Kami（あめのいわくすぶねのかみ） ==== History ==== It is said that Prince Koretaka, after defeating three giant kites that attacked children, built a shrine in Ogura on his way back from Kawachi. Since then, it came to be revered as a protector of children, thus "Komori Shrine." Today, the main shrine Ogura Shrine is often mistaken for Komori Shrine, but Komori Shrine exists separately as a precinct shrine within Ogura Shrine{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=284 |isbn=}}. === Monument of Gyokuro Tea Origin === Within the precincts is a "Monument of the Origin of Gyokuro Tea Production," based on an anecdote about the birth of {{ill|lt=Gyokuro|Q1424874|WD=Q1424874|1=Gyokuro}} by Yamamoto Kahei{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/nosan/documents/1312184290019.pdf|title=Tour of the Kyoto-Yamashiro Uji tea region|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2022-06-13}}. === Gallery === Image:Ogura komainu 02.jpg|Cover hall（honden inside） Image:Ogura Jinja Uji Honden.jpg|Honden Image:Ogura komainu 01.jpg|Ogura Shrine komainu Image:Ogura Shrine 20190309.jpg|Precincts and torii of Ogura Shrine Image:Komori Shrine Torii 20190309.jpg|Torii of Komori Shrine === Address === Kyoto Prefecture, Uji City, Ogura-chō Terauchi 31{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=Origins of Komori Shrine |publisher=Komori Shrine office |year= |page= |isbn=}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: about 10 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Ogura Station (Kyoto)|Q7080297|WD=Q7080297|lt=Ogura Station|1=Ogura Station}}. * {{ill|lt=JR Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}}: about 14 minutes on foot north from {{ill|lt=JR Ogura Station|Q1049411|WD=Q1049411|1=JR Ogura Station}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Komori Shrine|Q11447878|WD=Q11447878|1=Komori Shrine}} == Categories == Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:宇治市の建築物]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おぐらじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8130005006880 == Commons category (P373) == * Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府宇治市小倉町寺内31', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 巨椋神社 {{commons category|Category:Ogura-jinja (Uji, Kyoto)}} [[JA:巨椋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q63148006}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}Ogura-chō, Terauchi 31| |画像=[[File:Ogura_Jinja_Uji_Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |創建=Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|n|6027901792}} [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ogura Shrine'''（おぐらじんじゃ）is a shrine in Terauchi, Ogura-chō, {{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is thought to have been the clan shrine of the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji, and is said to have begun when they enshrined their ancestral deity{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Kunihiko Shimonaka |title=Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1981/03/13 |page=108 |isbn=}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}（たけみかづちのかみ） * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}（ふつぬしのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}（あめのこやねのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}}（ひめのおおかみ） * Tutelary deity of the Ogura area === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}, but it is more likely that it originated when the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji enshrined its ancestral deity. Ogura-no-Muraji is an ancient clan listed under "Divine descent" in the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}. === Outline === In the late Heian period it became a place of worship for the {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|WD=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}, became a Kasuga-line shrine, and was called Kasuga Shrine until early modern times. There is a precinct shrine, Komori Shrine (Uji). It originally stood at Kasuga-no-mori in Ogura-chō, and was later moved to its current location{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=283 |isbn=}}. === Precincts === In the precincts are giant hackberry and zelkova trees said to be at least 700-800 years old{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 6: Life and Environment in Western Uji River Area |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1981/03/01 |page=514 |isbn=}}. === Komori Shrine === [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Keidaisha Komori Jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Komori Shrine honden}}]] ==== Enshrined deities ==== Hiruko-no-Kami（ひるこのかみ） Ame-no-Iwakusubune-no-Kami（あめのいわくすぶねのかみ） ==== History ==== It is said that Prince Koretaka, after defeating three giant kites that attacked children, built a shrine in Ogura on his way back from Kawachi. Since then, it came to be revered as a protector of children, thus "Komori Shrine." Today, the main shrine Ogura Shrine is often mistaken for Komori Shrine, but Komori Shrine exists separately as a precinct shrine within Ogura Shrine{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=284 |isbn=}}. === Monument of Gyokuro Tea Origin === Within the precincts is a "Monument of the Origin of Gyokuro Tea Production," based on an anecdote about the birth of {{ill|lt=Gyokuro|Q1424874|WD=Q1424874|1=Gyokuro}} by Yamamoto Kahei{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/nosan/documents/1312184290019.pdf|title=Tour of the Kyoto-Yamashiro Uji tea region|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2022-06-13}}. === Gallery === Image:Ogura komainu 02.jpg|Cover hall（honden inside） Image:Ogura Jinja Uji Honden.jpg|Honden Image:Ogura komainu 01.jpg|Ogura Shrine komainu Image:Ogura Shrine 20190309.jpg|Precincts and torii of Ogura Shrine Image:Komori Shrine Torii 20190309.jpg|Torii of Komori Shrine === Address === Kyoto Prefecture, Uji City, Ogura-chō Terauchi 31{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=Origins of Komori Shrine |publisher=Komori Shrine office |year= |page= |isbn=}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: about 10 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Ogura Station (Kyoto)|Q7080297|WD=Q7080297|lt=Ogura Station|1=Ogura Station}}. * {{ill|lt=JR Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}}: about 14 minutes on foot north from {{ill|lt=JR Ogura Station|Q1049411|WD=Q1049411|1=JR Ogura Station}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Komori Shrine|Q11447878|WD=Q11447878|1=Komori Shrine}} == Categories == shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:宇治市の建築物]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]

(Uji, Kyoto)|ja|巨椋神社}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Ogura Shrine |所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}Ogura-chō, Terauchi 31| |画像=[[File:Ogura_Jinja_Uji_Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |創建=Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |例祭={{ill|lt=October 1|Q2913|WD=Q2913|1=October 1}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Ogura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|n|6027901792}} [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ogura Shrine'''（おぐらじんじゃ）is a shrine in Terauchi, Ogura-chō, {{ill|lt=Uji|Q326956|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinai small shrine|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is thought to have been the clan shrine of the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji, and is said to have begun when they enshrined their ancestral deity{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Kunihiko Shimonaka |title=Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture |publisher=Heibonsha |year=1981/03/13 |page=108 |isbn=}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}（たけみかづちのかみ） * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}（ふつぬしのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}（あめのこやねのかみ） * {{ill|lt=Hime-ōkami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime-ōkami|1=Himegami}}（ひめのおおかみ） * Tutelary deity of the Ogura area === History === === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, it was founded by imperial order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sushun|Q316654|WD=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}, but it is more likely that it originated when the ancient clan Ogura-no-Muraji enshrined its ancestral deity. Ogura-no-Muraji is an ancient clan listed under "Divine descent" in the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}. === Outline === In the late Heian period it became a place of worship for the {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|WD=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}, became a Kasuga-line shrine, and was called Kasuga Shrine until early modern times. There is a precinct shrine, Komori Shrine (Uji). It originally stood at Kasuga-no-mori in Ogura-chō, and was later moved to its current location{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=283 |isbn=}}. === Precincts === In the precincts are giant hackberry and zelkova trees said to be at least 700-800 years old{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 6: Life and Environment in Western Uji River Area |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1981/03/01 |page=514 |isbn=}}. === Komori Shrine === [[ファイル:Ogura Jinja Uji Keidaisha Komori Jinja.jpg|thumb|150px|{{center|Komori Shrine honden}}]] ==== Enshrined deities ==== Hiruko-no-Kami（ひるこのかみ） Ame-no-Iwakusubune-no-Kami（あめのいわくすぶねのかみ） ==== History ==== It is said that Prince Koretaka, after defeating three giant kites that attacked children, built a shrine in Ogura on his way back from Kawachi. Since then, it came to be revered as a protector of children, thus "Komori Shrine." Today, the main shrine Ogura Shrine is often mistaken for Komori Shrine, but Komori Shrine exists separately as a precinct shrine within Ogura Shrine{{Cite book|和書|author=edited by Tatsusaburō Hayashiya and Kenzirō Fujioka |title=History of Uji City 1: Ancient History and Landscape |publisher=Uji City Office |year=1974/07/01 |page=284 |isbn=}}. === Monument of Gyokuro Tea Origin === Within the precincts is a "Monument of the Origin of Gyokuro Tea Production," based on an anecdote about the birth of {{ill|lt=Gyokuro|Q1424874|WD=Q1424874|1=Gyokuro}} by Yamamoto Kahei{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/nosan/documents/1312184290019.pdf|title=Tour of the Kyoto-Yamashiro Uji tea region|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2022-06-13}}. === Gallery === Image:Ogura komainu 02.jpg|Cover hall（honden inside） Image:Ogura Jinja Uji Honden.jpg|Honden Image:Ogura komainu 01.jpg|Ogura Shrine komainu Image:Ogura Shrine 20190309.jpg|Precincts and torii of Ogura Shrine Image:Komori Shrine Torii 20190309.jpg|Torii of Komori Shrine === Address === Kyoto Prefecture, Uji City, Ogura-chō Terauchi 31{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=Origins of Komori Shrine |publisher=Komori Shrine office |year= |page= |isbn=}} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}}: about 10 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Ogura Station (Kyoto)|Q7080297|WD=Q7080297|lt=Ogura Station|1=Ogura Station}}. * {{ill|lt=JR Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}}: about 14 minutes on foot north from {{ill|lt=JR
